passing_score_scale: Scaled score; passing threshold determined by NCCPA and varies by exam form
exam_format: Linear fixed-form computer-based test (CBT); NBME item development
exam_price_notes: $475 exam fee; rescheduling fees may apply.

testing_centers.notes: PANCE and PANRE administered at Pearson VUE test centers; PANRE-LA is online quarterly modules

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name must match NCCPA registration exactly. Secondary ID may be requested.
exam_rules: Closed-book computer-based test. NBME-developed content; multiple-choice only (single best answer). PANCE: 5 blocks of 60 questions, 60 minutes per block, optional 45-minute total break. PANRE: 4 blocks of 60 questions, 60 minutes per block, optional 45-minute total break. On-screen calculator provided. No personal items permitted in testing room.
renewal_options: Initial PA-C certification valid for 6 years. Ongoing Maintenance of Certification (MOC) required every 10 years thereafter. MOC includes: 100 CME credits per 2-year cycle (50 must be Category 1), passing PANRE or completing PANRE-LA, and logging practice hours. Biennial application submitted through NCCPA portal.

prerequisites.description: Graduate of an ARC-PA accredited physician assistant program; degree required

retake_policy.notes: PANCE: maximum 6 lifetime attempts total. After failing 6 times, candidate is permanently ineligible for PANCE. PANRE: no stated lifetime maximum; must be taken within MOC 10-year cycle. Full exam fee required for each attempt.

official_study_resources.title: PANCE/PANRE Blueprint
official_study_resources.notes: Free downloadable content blueprint listing organ systems and task areas with percentage weights

official_study_resources.title: NCCPA Practice Exam
official_study_resources.notes: Official NCCPA practice exam available through NCCPA portal

aliases: pance physician assistant national certifying exam
